topIssues.title: fix(cloud): dedicated agents fall back to local_inference → 'provider issue' on every chat (cloud text-inference not wired in container)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

Every chat to a **dedicated cloud agent** returns the canned fallback:
```json
{"text":"Sorry, I'm having a provider issue","agentName":"Eliza","failureKind":"local_inference"}
```
in ~130ms, on every message. `failureKind: "local_inference"` (`classifyChatFailure` → `isLocalInferenceError`, `packages/agent/src/api/chat-routes.ts:1528`) means the agent **attempted local/on-device inference and errored** instead of using the cloud inference plane. For a dedicated *cloud* agent that should never happen — it implies cloud text-generation isn't wired in the container, so it falls back to a local model that isn't present (or hits ENOSPC).

`isLocalInferenceError` matches: `local inference | local model | on-device | device bridge | llama | gguf | no local model | model download | enospc | no space left | disk full`.

## Launch impact

**P1 / likely launch blocker.** If this reproduces on every freshly-provisioned dedicated agent, no dedicated-agent chat works (the user-visible symptom is the phone showing "Sorry, I'm having a provider issue" forever). Confirmed live on-device (Seeker) + via the management API.

## Repro

1. Provision a dedicated agent (`POST /api/v1/eliza/agents {agentName, dockerImage:"ghcr.io/elizaos/eliza:sha-7032a6a", autoProvision:true}`) → it reaches `status: running`, healthy heartbeat, base `200`.
2. `POST /api/v1/eliza/agents/:id/api/conversations/:cid/messages {text:"hi"}` → `200` with `{"text":"Sorry, I'm having a provider issue","failureKind":"local_inference"}`.

## What's ruled OUT (already chased)

- **Stale image** — no. A brand-new agent on the **current** prod image (`sha-7032a6a`) fails identically. (Pre-existing agents were on stale immutable images `sha-2e08c1d`/`sha-7a5d4f9`, but image is not the cause.)
- **Revoked injected key** — the failure is `local_inference` (tried local), not an auth/cloud error, so it's not "cloud key 401 → fallback".
- **Tailnet/provisioning outage** — separate issue (CP `tailscaled` had died → fleet-wide provision wedge), already fixed; agents now provision + reach `200`. This `local_inference` failure is underneath that and persists after recovery.
- **#8769** (cloud containers use cloud **embeddings** not local gte-small) is **MERGED** — but that was embeddings; **text generation** still appears to attempt local inference here.

## Root-cause direction (needs agent-container env/logs — I don't have node SSH)

Likely the container's **text** model provider isn't pointed at cloud (missing `ELIZAOS_CLOUD_ENABLED` / `ELIZAOS_CLOUD_*_MODEL` overrides / cloud key for generation), so generation falls through to a local llama/gguf path that errors. Open questions for whoever has container access:
1. Is `ELIZAOS_CLOUD_ENABLED=true` + the cloud model overrides + cloud inference key actually injected into the agent container env at provision time?
2. Does this reproduce for an agent provisioned via the **normal app first-run / shared→dedicated handoff** flow, or only via the bare management-API create? (i.e. is the cloud-config injection create-path-dependent?)
3. The matcher also hits `enospc/disk full` — are the agent nodes (esp. `eliza-core-prod-2` / 178.63.251.122) low on disk? That would make a generation/model write fail with a disk error that classifies as `local_inference`.

Earlier this session a fresh agent passed `Say PONG` 20/20 on `sha-7032a6a`, so either something regressed or the working path differs from the one above.

## Affected
- nubs's dedicated agents (now re-provisioned to a single fresh `316438e9` on `sha-7032a6a`, healthy infra, still failing `local_inference`).

Related: #8769 (merged), tracker #8434. Filed by phone-agent from on-device verification on Seeker.

topIssues.title: Voice: name-aware "hey <character>" wake word — activate voice + inline reply in transcript mode, battery-efficient cross-platform
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

Make **"hey <character name>"** a first-class, battery-efficient, cross-platform wake word that:

1. **Activates voice when it is not already on** — saying the wake word opens a listening window that stays open until the agent responds (already partially landed; see *Current state*).
2. **Works inside transcription mode** — while the app is recording a long-form transcript (replies suppressed), the wake word makes the agent **respond inline once** and the conversation **continues as a parallel chat inside the transcript**, without exiting transcription.
3. **Follows the character's name** — the user can rename the character in settings; the wake phrase tracks that name (`"hey eliza"` → rename to *Ada* → `"hey ada"`), zero config.
4. Uses **native acceleration on Android, iOS, Linux, macOS, Windows** and is cheap enough to run always-on on battery.

This issue captures the full design, the backend decision, the work breakdown, and the validation matrix (unit → state fuzz → real audio → iOS simulator → physical device), with recorded **video + audio** evidence.

---

## Current state (what already exists)

**Detection backends — two of them:**

- **openWakeWord** (`plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/wake-word.ts`, `wake-word-ggml.ts`) — a dedicated tiny GGML hotword model (mel filterbank → 96-dim embedding → per-phrase classifier head), fused into `libelizainference` (`eliza_inference_wakeword_*`). **~0.23 ms / 80 ms frame on native CPU**, native acceleration across all platforms via the fused lib. Default head `OPENWAKEWORD_DEFAULT_HEAD = "hey-eliza"` (`wake-word.ts:74`). A real "hey eliza" head has been trained and verified end-to-end; training pipeline lives in `packages/training/scripts/wakeword/` (`train_eliza1_wakeword_head.py`, `synthesize_wakeword_corpus.py` — **TTS-synthesized corpus**, `convert_openwakeword_to_gguf.py`). **Constraint: phrases are not zero-shot — each needs a trained classifier head.**
- **Swabble** (`plugins/plugin-native-swabble/`) — a Capacitor plugin that runs **continuous native OS ASR** (`SFSpeechRecognizer` / Android `SpeechRecognizer` / Web Speech / desktop Whisper.cpp) and fuzzy-matches the transcript against `triggers: string[]`. **Arbitrary phrases (incl. a renamed character) work zero-shot**, but continuous full ASR is **not battery-efficient for always-on**, and it already emits a typed `wakeWord` event (`{ wakeWord, command, transcript, postGap, confidence? }`).

**UI / orchestration already landed (PRs in this branch lineage):**
- `packages/ui/src/voice/wake-listen-window.ts` — pure state machine: `idle → open → awaiting-response → idle`, idle timeout + safety cap + re-arm.
- `packages/ui/src/voice/useWakeListenWindow.ts` — subscribes to the Swabble `wakeWord` event, drives the hands-free mic, stays inert under always-on.
- Wired into `packages/ui/src/components/shell/useShellController.ts`.
- Settings: `VoiceConfigView.tsx` `WakeWordSection` (triggers default hardcoded `["eliza"]`), `VoiceSection.tsx` wake toggle.

**Transcription mode (the surface feature 2 plugs into):**
- Client suppresses replies by *never sending* a transcription-intent final — it accumulates into `TranscriptSessionAccumulator` (`packages/ui/src/voice/transcript-session.ts`).
- Server safety net: `transcriptionModeActive(message)` + the `core.transcription_mode` evaluator (`packages/core/src/services/message.ts:1689,2939`) returns `IGNORE` + `clearReply` when `metadata.transcriptionMode === true`.
- `ContinuousChatOverlay` `ThreadLine` already renders interleaved user/assistant bubbles — an inline reply needs **no new UI**.

---

## Backend decision

| | openWakeWord (GGML, fused) | Swabble (native OS ASR) |
|---|---|---|
| Always-on battery | ✅ ~0.23 ms/frame, native CPU | ❌ continuous full ASR |
| Cross-platform incl. Linux/Windows desktop | ✅ fused `libelizainference` | ⚠️ Electrobun/Whisper bridge only |
| Native acceleration | ✅ Metal/Vulkan/CUDA/CPU | OS ASR (mobile), CPU desktop |
| Arbitrary/renamed phrase zero-shot | ❌ needs trained head | ✅ |
| Already wired into voice pipeline | ✅ engine.ts | ❌ opt-in, unwired into pipeline |

**Decision — a two-stage name-aware wake:**

- **Stage A (always-on, cheap, battery):** openWakeWord generic detector (or native VAD) runs continuously. This is the only thing burning power at idle.
- **Stage B (confirmation, on candidate):** when Stage A fires, open a **short ASR window** (we already have STT/ASR + fused transcription) and **fuzzy-match the transcript against the current character name** (`"hey <name>"`). This gives **zero-shot, rename-following** name support while keeping idle cost at Stage-A levels.
- **Head fast-path:** if a trained head exists for the current name (shipped `hey-eliza`, or auto-trained via `synthesize_wakeword_corpus.py` + `train_eliza1_wakeword_head.py`), Stage B is skipped — pure-openWakeWord, lowest latency.
- **Swabble** stays as the **fallback detector** where the fused FFI is unavailable (e.g. some browsers), reusing its existing `wakeWord` event.

This unifies both backends behind one `WakeController` whose default phrase is **derived from the character name** (`s.characterData?.name` → `agentStatus?.agentName` → `"eliza"`, per `App.tsx:1523`).

---

## Work breakdown

### A. Name-aware triggers
- Derive the default wake trigger from the live character name (`useAppSelector(s => s.characterData?.name ...)`), so renames propagate. `VoiceConfigView.tsx` default `["eliza"]` becomes name-derived.
- A pure **name-matcher** (`packages/ui/src/voice/wake-name-match.ts`): normalizes + fuzzy-matches `"hey <name>"`, `"<name>"`, `"hey, <name>"`, handles homophones/ASR slop (Levenshtein ≤ threshold), exposed for Stage-B confirmation and Swabble triggers.
- On rename, update the persisted Swabble `triggers` if they still equal the old name (don't clobber a user-customized phrase).

### B. Transcript-mode inline reply ("parallel chat")
- In `useShellController` transcription branch: when an utterance **matches the wake phrase**, send it as a **reply-eliciting** turn (`VOICE_DM`, *without* `metadata.transcriptionMode`) instead of silently accumulating — the agent replies and speaks once.
- Record both the user's wake turn and the assistant reply **into the transcript session** as speaker-labeled segments, so the parallel chat appears inline in the transcript and the finalized record.
- Transcription **does not exit** — the re-listen loop resumes recording after the reply.
- New pure helper + `transcription-exit.ts`-style detection so it is unit-testable.

### C. Wake activation when voice is off
- Already landed via `wake-listen-window` / `useWakeListenWindow`; extend so the same wake event drives (a) open-mic-if-off, and (b) the transcript inline-reply path when transcription is active.

### D. Detection pipeline (`WakeController`)
- Stage A openWakeWord/VAD always-on → Stage B ASR-confirm vs character name → emit unified wake event.
- Head fast-path + Swabble fallback selection by platform/capability.
- Optional: auto-train a head for the current name via the existing TTS corpus pipeline (follow-up; gated by training availability).

---

## Testing, validation & verification matrix

| Tier | What | How |
|---|---|---|
| **Unit** | `wake-listen-window` reducer, `wake-name-match` (homophones, accents, slop), transcript inline-reply branch, name-rename propagation | vitest, frozen clock |
| **State fuzz** | random event sequences over the wake + transcription state machines (wake during open/awaiting/transcription/always-on, double-wake, exit-phrase races) — assert invariants (mic never stuck open, transcription never silently exits, exactly-one inline reply per wake) | property/fuzz test (mirrors `ContinuousChatOverlay.fuzz.test.tsx`) |
| **Real audio (synthesized)** | TTS renders "hey <name>" → openWakeWord scores the PCM → assert detection; TTS→ASR→name-match round-trip; barge-in latency | extend `plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/e2e-harness.test.ts` |
| **Real audio (acoustic loop)** | play "hey eliza" through the **Mac speakers**, capture via the **Mac microphone**, assert wake fires end-to-end | host harness + ffmpeg avfoundation capture |
| **iOS simulator** | boot the app in the booted iPhone 16 Pro simulator, drive the wake/transcript UI, screen-record | `xcrun simctl` + app build |
| **Physical device** | install on the paired iPhone 16 Pro Max, exercise on-device openWakeWord + mic, screen-record with audio | device build + `xcrun devicectl` |
| **Recorded evidence** | **video WITH audio** of each tier passing | ffmpeg screen+mic capture (Mac), simulator/device screen recording; artifacts under `.github/issue-evidence/` |

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Wake phrase defaults to the character name and updates on rename (test).
- [ ] Saying the wake word with the mic off opens a listening window that closes once the agent responds (test + video).
- [ ] In transcription mode, the wake word yields exactly one inline agent reply (spoken), recorded into the transcript, **without exiting transcription** (test + video).
- [ ] Idle always-on detection runs on the battery-efficient openWakeWord path; Swabble only as fallback (documented + measured).
- [ ] Cross-platform: validated on macOS host + iOS simulator + physical iPhone; Android/Linux/Windows paths documented and unit-covered.
- [ ] State-fuzz suite green; mic-never-stuck and exactly-one-reply invariants hold.
- [ ] Recorded video+audio evidence attached for unit/real-audio/sim/device tiers.

---

## Non-goals (this issue)

- Shipping trained wake heads for every possible name (auto-train pipeline is a follow-up; Stage-B ASR covers arbitrary names today).
- Re-architecting TTS/ASR — reuse the existing fused stack.

topIssues.title: Planner confabulation/exhaustion: 3 structural root-cause follow-ups (multi-bot arena)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
Follow-ups from the arena confabulation incident (prompt+routing patches shipped in the linked PR). These are the deeper *structural* causes, deferred deliberately rather than patched:

1. **Don't force `requiresTool` on bot-to-bot crosstalk.** The false-ack seed fired because the simple→requiresTool promotion ran on a message addressed to *another bot* (not the agent/owner). Suppress the promotion when the inbound message's addressee is a non-owner bot.

2. **Reaction-stub truncation drops message content in context.** A reacted-to message was reduced to `*Added <emoji> to: "…"*` in the planner context, so the model fused a dangling ack with a truncated user statement and back-rationalized a task. Context-build should preserve the reacted message's text.

3. **Safe forced-tool-exhaustion reply (deferred Fix 2).** When a turn is forced to call a tool the model legitimately can't satisfy, it currently errors into a generic 'hit a snag'. Surfacing the planner's free-text is unsafe (native `raw.text` can be a pre-tool thought — guarded by the `does not capture native text fallback` test). A safe version must gate through `userSafeFinalMessage` (rejects reasoning/leak/fabrication) before surfacing.

These are the durable class fixes; the shipped PR neutralizes the *observed* failure.

topIssues.title: fix(cloud-frontend): /app-auth/authorize crashes — useAuth() called outside a populated <StewardProvider auth=…> (blocks all monetized-app sign-in)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

The Eliza Cloud OAuth authorize page (`/app-auth/authorize`) crashes for unauthenticated visitors with:

> Something went wrong — `useAuth must be used within a <StewardProvider> with an `auth` prop.`

This blocks **all** monetized-app OAuth sign-in: a new user who clicks "sign in with Eliza Cloud" on any deployed monetized app can never complete auth. P0 for the monetized-app / 10-user launch.

## Repro (live)

1. Deploy a monetized app (e.g. the agent-home `text-my-ex` app) — registered, `appId` valid, `/api/config` returns it.
2. Open the app, click **Sign in with Eliza Cloud**.
3. Redirects to `https://elizacloud.ai/app-auth/authorize?app_id=<uuid>&redirect_uri=<app>&state=<s>`.
4. Page renders the error boundary: *"useAuth must be used within a `<StewardProvider>` with an `auth` prop."*

## Root cause

- `packages/ui/src/cloud-ui/components/auth/authorize-content.tsx:3` imports `{ StewardLogin, useAuth } from "@stwd/react"` and calls `useAuth()` (~line 75).
- `@stwd/react`'s `useAuth()` requires an ancestor `<StewardProvider auth={…}>` with a **non-null `auth` prop**.
- The `/app-auth/authorize` route is registered as a **public page**: `packages/ui/src/cloud/public-pages/register.ts:160` (`path: "app-auth/authorize"`).
- `<StewardProvider>` is only rendered with a populated `auth` prop inside the **authenticated** shell: `packages/ui/src/cloud/shell/StewardProviderRuntime.tsx:270`.
- An unauthenticated visitor on the public authorize route therefore has no `<StewardProvider auth=…>` ancestor → `useAuth()` throws → error boundary.

The authorize page is intrinsically a *public, pre-auth* page that nonetheless depends on the Steward auth context to drive the OAuth grant — that's the architectural mismatch.

## Proposed fix direction

Render the public `/app-auth/authorize` route inside a `<StewardProvider>` seeded with a **public/unauthenticated** Steward auth instance (the same provider-discovery path the login page already uses — see `packages/ui/src/cloud/public-pages/pages/login/steward-login-section.tsx`), so `useAuth()` resolves on public pages. Apply to any public page that calls `@stwd/react` hooks. Verify via the `audit:cloud` loop + a real OAuth round-trip from a deployed app.

## Lane

cloud-frontend (`packages/ui/src/cloud*`), deployed to `elizacloud.ai` — cloud-agent owns the fix + deploy. Filed from vps-agent (root-caused while testing the monetized-app build flow end-to-end). Linked from launch tracker #8434.

topIssues.title: orchestrator: no live multi-account CI, untestable timing code, and a 3,931-line UI monolith — make the coding orchestrator verifiable end-to-end
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

The coding orchestrator (`plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator`) is feature-complete on paper but is **not verified end-to-end with real Claude/Codex accounts in CI**, and its reliability rests on a deep mat of ad-hoc timer/race/orphan-recovery code that is the dominant flakiness source. The multi-account selection brain (`account-pool.ts`) and the spawn-injection bridge (`coding-account-bridge.ts`) are well built and unit-tested — but every "rotation across ≥2 real accounts of each provider" claim is asserted **only against a fake `acpx` binary**. Real OAuth refresh, real per-account `CODEX_HOME`/`auth.json` materialization, and real subscription auth have zero automated proof.

This matters now because the orchestrator is the spine of every coding-sub-agent flow, and we cannot ship it as "rock-solid" while (a) its lifecycle/loop-guard logic is only unit-tested in pieces and never under concurrent multi-task load, (b) there is no CI lane that spawns a real Claude or Codex subscription, (c) there is no mechanism to feed refreshable account credentials into GitHub Actions, and (d) the account-connect surface is a desktop Settings dialog disjoint from the orchestrator, so a misconfigured pool silently degrades to single-account.

## Critical assessment (current state)

**1. Reliability debt is concentrated in untestable timing code.** The spawn/route layer is dominated by ad-hoc retry, orphan reconciliation, SIGTERM grace windows, and loop-guards: 50 `setTimeout`/`setInterval` sites across the plugin; `acp-service.ts` has 36 catch blocks and `sub-agent-router.ts` 33; 108 `flaky|retry|race|loop|best-effort|eventually|debounce|backoff` markers in those two files alone. The router carries **two independent runaway-loop backstops** (`DEFAULT_ROUND_TRIP_CAP`, 32; `DEFAULT_STATE_LOST_RESPAWN_CAP`, 3) plus a manual "roll the round-trip counter back when a `task_complete` is suppressed" reconciliation and a documented TOCTOU window. AcpService reconciles "stale orphans" on start and resumes orphaned sessions with a grace window for sessions that "may not have written state yet." `codex-auth.ts` adds its own 30s stale-lock + 30-retry file lock around the OAuth token cache. None of these timing paths is exercised by a deterministic test under load — they are the textbook origin of intermittent failures.

**2. Multi-account E2E exists but is not in CI and has no credential feed.** `grep` of `.github/workflows` confirms the offline composed E2E (`test:e2e:multi-account`), the live smoke (`ORCHESTRATOR_LIVE=1`), and the live multi-account gate (`ORCHESTRATOR_LIVE_MULTI_ACCOUNT=1`) appear in **zero** workflows. The only scheduled orchestrator job runs a single `GROQ_API_KEY` model — it never spawns a real Claude or Codex subscription. There is also no mechanism to feed refreshable credentials into Actions: `codex-auth.ts` holds the OAuth client id + refresh logic, but nothing exports/refreshes those tokens into CI secrets.

**3. The account login UI is a Settings dialog, not a test/E2E harness, and is disjoint from the orchestrator.** `AddAccountDialog.tsx` (rendered inside `AccountList.tsx`, which `settings/ProviderPanels.tsx` mounts) is real and reachable — but it is surfaced nowhere in the orchestrator views. There is no "log in with N accounts, confirm ≥1 Codex + ≥1 Claude connected and healthy, export for CI" flow. `selectCodingAccount()` returns `null` and silently falls back to single-account when the bridge/account is absent, so a misconfigured pool is invisible.

**4. The orchestrator UI is a 3,931-line monolith.** `OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx` (3,931 lines) blocks the mandated screenshot/manual-review loop and makes per-account / per-session status, stall/force-stop state, and account-health surfacing hard to add or verify.

Net: the architecture is sound and the ideal is documented; the gap is **verification**.

## Evidence in code

- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/acp-service.ts:456` — orphan-recovery grace window: "a freshly-spawned session may not have written its state file yet"; timing-dependent reconciliation on every start. 36 catch blocks in this file.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts:371` — documented TOCTOU window: "two retry sessions both passed the check" in the verify-retry handoff path. 33 catch blocks in this file.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts:697` — manual "roll the round-trip counter back when a `task_complete` event is suppressed" — the heart of the runaway-loop guard.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts:41-42` / `:452-453` / `:478` — `DEFAULT_ROUND_TRIP_CAP=32` and `DEFAULT_STATE_LOST_RESPAWN_CAP=3`, held as separate per-session state (`roundTripCap` / `stateLostRespawnCap`) with an env override (`ACPX_SUB_AGENT_ROUND_TRIP_CAP` at `:478`): two independent loop backstops.
- `plugins/plugin-codex-cli/src/codex-auth.ts:8-11` — `OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`, `LOCK_STALE_MS=30_000`, `LOCK_RETRY_MAX=30`; the OAuth client id + refresh logic a CI credential-refresh harness must drive (and another concurrency surface).
- `packages/app-core/src/services/account-pool.ts:152` — `AccountPool.select`: strategy + session affinity + burst-spread (1,078-line file). Well-built selection brain, only unit-tested, never under live multi-task load.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/coding-account-selection.ts:103` / `:148-167` — `MULTI_ACCOUNT_AGENT_TYPES = {claude, codex, opencode}`; `selectCodingAccount()` doc states "Returns null (single-account fallback)" and the body returns `null` on missing bridge / catch / no account — no readiness assertion.
- `packages/app-core/src/services/coding-account-bridge.ts:245-254` — `buildEnvPatch`: claude→`CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, codex→per-account `CODEX_HOME`/`auth.json` (materialized at `:173-203`). The spawn-injection contract that needs live proof.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/scripts/compose-multi-account-e2e.ts:10` / `:47-66` — offline composed E2E: real `AccountPool` + bridge + `AcpService.spawnSession`, but "the only stand-in is the coding-agent binary itself (a tiny fake 'acpx')." The only automated multi-account proof — and it stubs the actual CLI.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/__tests__/unit/multi-account-spawn.test.ts:323` — "rotates consecutive spawns across distinct accounts (least-used)" using a mocked `NativeAcpClient` (`:55`); proves wiring, not real auth.
- `.github/workflows/benchmark-orchestrator-scheduled.yml:64`,`:71-76` — the only scheduled orchestrator CI run uses a single `GROQ_API_KEY` with a secret-presence gate; no Claude/Codex subscription, no pool, no rotation. `grep` for `ORCHESTRATOR_LIVE` / `test:e2e:multi-account` / `CODEX_HOME` / `saveAccount` across `.github/workflows` = **zero matches**.
- `packages/agent/src/api/accounts-routes.ts:553` / `:598` — `/api/accounts/*` HTTP surface (oauth start/status-SSE/cancel, api-key add, refresh-usage). Login plumbing exists, but no CI/test-harness consumer confirms ≥1 Codex + ≥1 Claude healthy and exports for Actions.
- `packages/ui/src/components/accounts/AccountList.tsx:157` — renders `AddAccountDialog`; `AccountList` is mounted in `packages/ui/src/components/settings/ProviderPanels.tsx:242`,`:312`, surfaced in **no** orchestrator view.
- `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx` — 3,931-line orchestrator UI monolith; no per-account/per-session health surfacing; too large for the mandated `audit:app` loop.
- `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/docs/multi-account-orchestration.md:14-22` — marks every layer (pool, OAuth flows, REST API, settings UI, orchestrator) "✅", but neither the live E2E nor the composed offline E2E is in CI, so the doc overstates verification posture.

## Scope & work order

High-confidence first.

- [ ] **Reliability — single lifecycle state machine.** Consolidate the two runaway-loop backstops in `sub-agent-router.ts` (`roundTripCap` + `stateLostRespawnCap`, the manual counter roll-back at `:697`, and the TOCTOU at `:371`) into one explicit pure transition function, modeled the way the supervisor/watchdog already do pure `runSupervisorTick` (`task-supervisor-service.ts:88`) / `detectStalledSessions` (`task-watchdog-service.ts:45`). Add a fuzz/property test asserting no event ordering produces a double-post, an early force-stop, or a leaked session.
- [ ] **Multi-account CI lane (additive, secret-gated).** Add a workflow that runs `test:e2e:multi-account` on every PR (fake `acpx`, zero secrets) AND a scheduled `ORCHESTRATOR_LIVE_MULTI_ACCOUNT=1` job against ≥1 real Codex + ≥1 real Claude account, skipping cleanly (exit 0 + `::notice::`) when secrets are absent — copy `benchmark-orchestrator-scheduled.yml`'s secret-presence gate (`:71-76`).
- [ ] **Credential feed into Actions.** Build a credential-refresh path that exports the connected Claude (`CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`) and Codex (`CODEX_HOME` `auth.json`: access/refresh/account_id) blobs into GH Actions secrets and refreshes them on a schedule, reusing the refresh logic in `codex-auth.ts` + `packages/agent/src/auth/oauth-flow.ts`. Store the minimal account JSON the pool reads so CI can seed 2× Claude + 2× Codex into a temp state dir and run real rotation.
- [ ] **Account-readiness harness.** Add a CLI/route that asserts the pool has ≥1 healthy Codex AND ≥1 healthy Claude (≥2 each for local rotation) using `coding-account-bridge`'s per-provider total/enabled/healthy report. Fail loudly instead of the silent single-account fallback in `coding-account-selection.ts:148-167`.
- [ ] **Sub-agent / room / task / multi-task correctness tests** (fake transport; template = the `multi-account-spawn.test.ts` `NativeAcpClient` mock at `:55`): (1) spawn→route→`task_complete`→evaluator summary; (2) room message routed to active sub-agent via `interruption-decider.ts` (deliver/queue/interrupt/ignore); (3) multi-task supervisor digest correctness across rooms; (4) concurrent multi-task isolation — two tasks, two accounts, distinct sessions, no cross-talk, correct round-trip accounting.
- [ ] **Orchestrator UI (medium).** Decompose `OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx` into a live-data wrapper + presentational sub-views, then add a per-account/per-session panel surfacing selected account, account health (ok/rate-limited/needs-reauth/invalid from `AccountPool`), stall/force-stop state, and a connect-accounts entry point (reuse `AccountList`/`AddAccountDialog`). Run the `audit:app` screenshot + manual-review loop.
- [ ] **Error handling (medium).** Audit the 36 catch blocks in `acp-service.ts` and 33 in `sub-agent-router.ts`; collapse log-and-continue swallows that mask spawn/auth failures into explicit surfaced errors — propagate the pool's existing needs-reauth / rate-limited / invalid distinctions instead of best-effort no-ops.

## Acceptance criteria

- A property/fuzz test over the consolidated router state machine passes: no event ordering yields a double-post, early force-stop, or leaked session.
- `test:e2e:multi-account` runs on every PR (no secrets, fake `acpx`) and is green.
- A scheduled live lane spawns ≥1 real Codex + ≥1 real Claude subscription, rotates across distinct accounts, and skips cleanly (exit 0 + `::notice::`) when secrets are absent.
- A credential-refresh job keeps the CI account blobs live and is proven by a green live run after a scheduled refresh.
- The readiness harness fails loudly when fewer than ≥1 healthy Codex + ≥1 healthy Claude are present; no silent single-account fallback.
- The four sub-agent/room/task/multi-task correctness tests exist and pass deterministically against a fake transport.
- `OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx` is decomposed; the orchestrator surface shows per-account health + pool readiness; `audit:app` produces a `good` verdict for the touched views.

## Required evidence (per PR_EVIDENCE.md)

- **Real-LLM trajectory** — REQUIRED for the live lane: a scenario-runner / live-orchestrator run against real Claude + Codex subscriptions showing distinct-account rotation (JSON report + native jsonl).
- **Backend logs** — REQUIRED: structured `[ClassName]` logs showing real per-account `CODEX_HOME`/`auth.json` materialization, `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` injection, and the readiness assertion firing.
- **Before/after screenshots + video walkthrough** — REQUIRED for the UI decomposition + account-health panel (`audit:app` output).
- **Audio walkthrough** — N/A (no voice/TTS/STT surface in scope).

## Related

- #9853 (cloud apps multi-tenant hardening — overlapping credential/secret handling)
- #9874 (planner confabulation follow-ups — orchestrator loop correctness)

This is a repo-wide reorg + 21 commits diverged from `develop`. Opening as a PR so CI validates the whole move before it lands, and so the reorg is reviewable. It supersedes the develop-only structure the current red CI jobs (Windows plugin-task-coordinator, Story Gate, Zero-Key, Server Tests) ran against — those lanes re-run here against the new layout.

topPRs.title: refactor(personal-assistant): replace LifeOpsService mixin god-class with namespaced sub-service composition
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## What

Replaces the 27-deep `LifeOpsService` class-mixin chain with **namespaced sub-service composition**. The mixin system is removed entirely.

### Before
`service.ts` composed 26 `withX` mixins via a 27-deep generic chain. TypeScript can't track type identity past ~6 chained generic mixins (TS2589 instantiation-depth), so every mixin carried an `as unknown as MixinClass<>` return cast and an `as unknown as <X>MixinDependencies` dependency cast; `service.ts` added a `Constructor<StatusMixinDependencies>` cast and 4 `Omit<>` collision deconflicts plus a `noUnsafeDeclarationMerging` interface merge. ~39 load-bearing casts in one package, hiding real cross-domain type drift.

### After
- **`LifeOpsContext`** (`lifeops-context.ts`) — the shared state/infra surface (runtime, repository, identity, ownership, generic audit, logging, events), derived via `Pick<LifeOpsServiceBase>` so it can't drift.
- **26 domain sub-services** under `lifeops/domains/*-service.ts`, each `class XDomain { constructor(ctx, deps) }` — cast-free, taking cross-domain dependencies as typed `deps` (no `this`-casts). The **browser↔screentime cycle** is broken via DI; the **reminders hub** (~5500 LOC) is one domain that its 5 consumers (definitions/goals/workflows/x/status) inject.
- **`LifeOpsService`** is now a single **concrete** `class extends LifeOpsServiceBase` holding the 26 domains, 263 flat delegators (preserving every existing call site byte-identically), and 26 typed **namespace getters** (`service.goals.x()`, `service.gmail.y()`, …). A concrete class surfaces every method directly, so the interface-merge, the 4 `Omit<>`s, and all assembly casts are gone.

## Why
The mixin god-class was not ideally factored: 22.9k LOC of 27 domains melted onto one class via a cast-laden inheritance chain whose casts were a genuine workaround for TS depth limits + method-name collisions. Composition removes the depth limit, the collisions, and the casts, and gives real domain separation.

## Result
- Repo-wide `as unknown as` **117 → 82** (−35); the plugin's mixin/assembly casts went from ~39 to **2** documented bridge casts for genuine pre-existing cross-package type drift (`LifeOpsGoalRecord` narrow re-decl; `LifeOpsDerivedEvent` vs `LifeOpsWorkflowEvent`). Domains are cast-free. Type-safety ratchet baseline lowered 117 → 82 to lock it in.
- **No behaviour change** — pure structural refactor; the flat public surface is preserved.

## Verification
- `tsgo` typecheck: **0 errors** in `src/lifeops/` (and `src/`).
- `vitest run src/lifeops/`: **16 files / 112 tests pass**.
- Each of the 8 commits is an independently-green slice (foundation → leaf domains → cross-dep domains → cycle break → reminders hub → dependent cluster → facade collapse → residual-cast cleanup).

topPRs.title: fix(app): harden desktop Cloud shell recovery
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens the desktop/mobile shell for Cloud-first startup and limited Cloud-agent bases:

- removes the separate native desktop pill window path; the in-canvas chat pill remains the single desktop surface
- wires external Cloud API bases into Electrobun renderers early and avoids local desktop RPC fallbacks for configured external bases
- recovers localhost/dev shells with stale local-backend state into first-run/Cloud selection instead of wedging on `Booting up...`
- gates full app-shell routes when the active base is a shared/dedicated Cloud chat adapter, avoiding unsupported `/api/views`, `/api/apps/runs`, `/api/workbench/todos`, `/api/approvals`, `/api/lifeops/inbox`, `/api/computer-use/approvals`, `/api/coding-agents`, and `/api/orchestrator/*` calls
- prevents localhost `:2138` Cloud sign-in from probing `/api/cloud/status` when no local backend exists
- polishes chat overlay, springboard/home ordering and image-backed tiles, route back behavior, and related startup failure UI

## Why

The reproduced browser logs were three separate problems:

1. Local Vite shell running without app-core behind it returned same-origin `/api/*` 502s.
2. A selected Cloud agent base is a limited chat adapter, not a full app-shell runtime, so desktop widgets/hydration were polling unsupported routes and creating CORS/404 noise.
3. The `My Eliza Cloud key isn't authorized for inference` message was traced in #8434 to a revoked key on a throwaway validation agent; that remains a cloud/phone provisioning self-heal follow-up, not a desktop UI route issue.

This PR fixes the desktop/UI-side causes and leaves the provisioning guard to the cloud/phone lane.

## Validation

- `bunx @biomejs/biome check packages/ui/src/api/app-shell-capabilities.ts packages/ui/src/api/app-shell-capabilities.test.ts packages/ui/src/hooks/useAvailableViews.ts packages/ui/src/hooks/useAvailableViews.test.tsx packages/ui/src/widgets/WidgetHost.tsx packages/ui/src/widgets/WidgetHost.test.tsx packages/ui/src/components/shell/ComputerUseApprovalOverlay.tsx packages/ui/src/first-run/use-first-run-controller.ts packages/ui/src/state/startup-phase-hydrate.ts packages/ui/src/state/useChatSend.ts packages/ui/src/state/useCloudState.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test -- src/api/app-shell-capabilities.test.ts src/hooks/useAvailableViews.test.tsx src/App.cloud-shell.test.tsx src/state/startup-coordinator.test.ts src/state/startup-phase-poll.test.ts src/state/startup-phase-restore.desktop-rpc.test.ts src/state/useChatLifecycle.readiness.test.ts src/widgets/WidgetHost.test.tsx src/state/useChatSend.test.tsx src/state/startup-phase-hydrate.view-interact.test.ts src/state/startup-phase-hydrate.navigate-frame.test.ts src/first-run/use-first-run-controller.remote.test.tsx src/first-run/use-first-run-controller.test.tsx src/first-run/use-first-run-controller.local.test.tsx` — 14 files / 137 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun test -- src/lifecycle/api-base-owner.test.ts` — 6 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test -- test/brand-surface.test.ts` — 10 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test:home-screen-e2e` — passed; generated artifacts cleaned from worktree
- `git diff --check`
- Playwright localhost smoke on `http://localhost:2138`:
  - fresh first-run shell rendered `Eliza Cloud / This device / Advanced` with no captured app-shell API failures
  - dedicated Cloud-agent base in localStorage produced no unsupported app-shell route polling
  - Cloud button click reached `Sign in to Eliza Cloud / Open sign-in page` with no `/api/cloud/status` 502

## Coordination Notes

- Tracked on #8434 as `[desktop-codex]`.
- #9842 is still open/unmerged at the time of this PR; prod reportedly runs that fix branch, but `develop` does not yet carry it.
- #9853 cloud apps hardening stack is separate and still has failing/pending checks.
